titles.en: Chapter on the Executor Withholding the heir's Wealth After Maturity, Leading Him to Commit Adultery Due to His Inability to Marry

text: 5526 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ اَلْكُلَيْنِيُّ رَضِيَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ عَمَّنْ رَوَاهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : فِي رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ أَوْصَى إِلَى رَجُلٍ وَ لَهُ اِبْنٌ صَغِيرٌ فَأَدْرَكَ اَلْغُلاَمُ وَ ذَهَبَ إِلَى اَلْوَصِيِّ فَقَالَ لَهُ رُدَّ عَلَيَّ مَالِي لِأَتَزَوَّجَ فَأَبَى عَلَيْهِ فَذَهَبَ حَتَّى زَنَى قَالَ "يُلْزَمُ ثُلُثَيْ إِثْمِ زِنَى هَذَا اَلرَّجُلِ ذَلِكَ اَلْوَصِيُّ اَلَّذِي مَنَعَهُ اَلْمَالَ وَ لَمْ يُعْطِهِ فَكَانَ يَتَزَوَّجُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5526 - Muhammad ibn Ya'qub Al-Kulayni, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, narrated from Muhammad ibn Yahya from Muhammad ibn Al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn Qays from someone who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said regarding a man who died and appointed someone as the guardian of his affairs while leaving behind a young son.
When the boy grew up, he went to the guardian and said: "Return my wealth to me so I can get married," but the guardian refused. The boy then went and committed adultery.
Abu Abdullah (as), said: "Two-thirds of the sin of this man's adultery is upon the guardian who withheld his wealth and did not give it to him, which would have allowed him to marry."
